name: U.S. Open
stakes: This U.S. Open proved a significant confidence booster for India, showcasing both a veteran's resurgence and the exciting potential of its next generation on the international stage.
summary:
India's contingent at the U.S. Open, a Super 300 event, concluded with a mix of promising breakthroughs and a strong return to form for a veteran. Kidambi Srikanth led the charge, reaching his first final of the season—a vital step in rebuilding momentum after a challenging period. While he ultimately faltered at the last hurdle, his deep run signals a positive trajectory for the former World No. 1.

The tournament also shone a spotlight on India's burgeoning young talent. Rounak Chouhan delivered a stunning performance, notably upsetting World No. 6 Chou Tien Chen on his way to the semifinals. Joining him in the last four was Devika Sihag, who also made waves by taking down the third seed. These impressive runs from Chouhan and Sihag underscore the depth and potential within the Indian badminton ecosystem, offering a glimpse into the future of the sport for the nation.

indiaContingent.name: Rounak Chouhan
indiaContingent.note: Stunned World No. 6 Chou Tien Chen
